title: HCI Storage Controller and Storage-Network Throughput Saturation Tracking
dataSources: PRIMARY multisearch normalization across VMware vSAN family: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder sourcetype vmware:perf for host disk.queueLatency.average, disk.deviceLatency.average, disk.maxQueueDepth.latest, net.usage.average, net.bytesTx.average, net.bytesRx.average style counters in your metric name field, plus PowerCLI scheduled export sourcetypes vmware:vsphere:host:hba and vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic for Get-VMHostStorage outstanding I/O and Get-VMHostNetworkAdapter link capacity and throughput. Nutanix: Splunk_TA_nutanix sourcetypes nutanix:host and nutanix:cluster_metrics plus custom HEC nutanix:host:throughput and nutanix:host:nic from Prism Element /PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/hosts/{uuid}/stats. Cisco HyperFlex: Splunk Add-on for Cisco HyperFlex when available or HX Connect REST modeled as hyperflex:node:controller and hyperflex:node:vnic. Azure Stack HCI: PowerShell export sourcetypes azure_stack_hci:storagebus and azure_stack_hci:vnic from Get-StorageBusBindingClient, Get-StorageBusCache, Get-NetAdapterStatistics, Get-SmbServerNetworkInterface. SECONDARY optional: Splunk Connect for SNMP sourcetype snmp:if:counters on storage-network ToR interfaces for ifHCInOctets, ifHCOutOctets, ifInDiscards, ifOutDiscards corroboration. Governance lookup hci_controller_owner.csv keyed on cluster_id and vendor.
app: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder + PowerCLI export; Splunk_TA_nutanix + custom HEC poller; Splunk Add-on for Cisco HyperFlex (or custom HEC); PowerShell export for Azure Stack HCI; (optional) Splunk Connect for SNMP for switch corroboration
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.11 HCI storage-controller queue AND storage-network pipe saturation: multisearch normalizes vSAN vmware:perf and PowerCLI vmware:vsphere:host:hba and vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic, Nutanix nutanix:host nutanix:cluster_metrics nutanix:host:throughput nutanix:host:nic, HyperFlex hyperflex:node:controller hyperflex:node:vnic, Azure Stack HCI azure_stack_hci:storagebus azure_stack_hci:vnic. Tunables: hci_controller_owner.csv columns target_max_controller_queue_pct target_max_link_util_pct target_max_p99_latency_us; owner_team; change_freeze_until freeze demotes non-critical to info. Window earliest=-25h@h latest=@h feeds perc99 AND streamstats window=288 on 5m bins (~24h rolling means). Alert cron */15 * * * *. Coalesce() covers camelCase drift. Relates to UC-19.2.3 guest-visible latency, UC-19.2.8 placement skew, UC-19.2.29 Nutanix CVM uplink slice, UC-19.2.30 vSAN component resync depth.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:perf" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:hba" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id, host_name, hostName, name, hostname, host, entity, HypervisorHostname))
      | eval vendor="vsan"
      | eval mkey=lower(toString(coalesce(metric, metric_name, MetricName, counter, "")))
      | eval vnum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, val, latest, "0")), 10)
      | eval disk_ql_ms=if(match(mkey, "disk\.queueLatency|queuelatency"), vnum, null())
      | eval disk_dl_ms=if(match(mkey, "disk\.deviceLatency|devicelatency"), vnum, null())
      | eval disk_mqd=if(match(mkey, "maxQueueDepth|maxqueuedepth"), vnum, null())
      | eval net_brx=if(match(mkey, "bytesrx|bytesRx"), vnum, null())
      | eval net_btx=if(match(mkey, "bytestx|bytesTx"), vnum, null())
      | eval disk_rd_iops=if(match(mkey, "numberreadaveraged|numberReadAveraged"), vnum, null())
      | eval disk_wr_iops=if(match(mkey, "numberwriteaveraged|numberWriteAveraged"), vnum, null())
      | eval hba_qd=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:hba", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(OutstandingIO, outstandingIo, outstanding_io, hba_outstanding_io, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval hba_qmax=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:hba", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(QueueDepth, queueDepth, max_queue_depth, hba_queue_max, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval ls_mbps=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(LinkSpeedMbps, linkSpeedMbps, link_speed_mbps, negotiated_speed_mbps, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vrx=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rx_mbps, rxMbps, net_rx_mbps, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vtx=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(tx_mbps, txMbps, net_tx_mbps, "0")), 10), null())
      | bin span=1m _time
      | stats max(disk_ql_ms) AS dql_ms max(disk_dl_ms) AS ddl_ms max(disk_mqd) AS dmqd max(net_brx) AS nbrx max(net_btx) AS nbtx max(disk_rd_iops) AS driops max(disk_wr_iops) AS dwiops max(hba_qd) AS hba_qd max(hba_qmax) AS hba_qmax max(ls_mbps) AS lsmb max(vrx) AS vrx max(vtx) AS vtx BY cluster_id node_id vendor _time
      | eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(hba_qd, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(hba_qmax, dmqd, controller_queue_max, "128")), 10)
      | eval controller_latency_us=coalesce(if((ddl_ms>0), ddl_ms*1000.0, null()), if((dql_ms>0), dql_ms*1000.0, null()))
      | eval kbps=coalesce(nbrx, 0)+coalesce(nbtx, 0)
      | eval vnic_throughput_mbps=round(coalesce(vrx, 0)+coalesce(vtx, 0)+if((kbps>0), (kbps*8.0)/1000.0, 0), 4)
      | eval controller_iops=coalesce(driops, 0)+coalesce(dwiops, 0)
      | eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=lsmb
      | fields cluster_id node_id vendor _time controller_iops controller_queue_depth controller_queue_max controller_latency_us vnic_throughput_mbps vnic_link_speed_mbps ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:host" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster_metrics" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host:throughput" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host:nic") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(host_uuid, uuid, hostUuid, name, host_name, node_id))
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | eval controller_iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_num_iops, controllerNumIops, controller_iops, iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_latency_us=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_avg_io_latency_usecs, controllerAvgIoLatencyUsecs, "0")), 10)
      | eval kbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_io_bandwidth_kBps, controllerIoBandwidthKBps, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_depth, controllerQueueDepth, io_queue_depth, ctrl_qd, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_max, controllerQueueMax, max_io_queue_depth, "128")), 10)
      | eval _tput=if((kbps>0), round((kbps/1024.0)*8.0, 4), null())
      | eval vnic_throughput_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_throughput_mbps, network_throughput_mbps, _tput, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(link_speed_mbps, linkSpeedMbps, negotiated_speed_mbps, nic_speed_mbps, "0")), 10)
      | fields cluster_id node_id vendor _time controller_iops controller_queue_depth controller_queue_max controller_latency_us vnic_throughput_mbps vnic_link_speed_mbps ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) (sourcetype="hyperflex:node:controller" OR sourcetype="hyperflex:node:vnic") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id, nodeId, node_name, host_name, serialNumber, serial))
      | eval vendor="hyperflex"
      | eval controller_iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_iops_used, controllerIopsUsed, controller_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_depth, controllerQueueDepth, ctrl_queue_depth, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_iops_max, controllerIopsMax, ctrl_queue_max, "128")), 10)
      | eval controller_latency_us=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_latency_us, controllerLatencyUs, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_throughput_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_throughput_mbps, vnicThroughputMbps, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_link_speed_mbps, vnicLinkSpeedMbps, "0")), 10)
      | fields cluster_id node_id vendor _time controller_iops controller_queue_depth controller_queue_max controller_latency_us vnic_throughput_mbps vnic_link_speed_mbps ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:storagebus" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:vnic") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_name, NodeName, NetAdapterName, ComputerName, name, "UNKNOWN_NODE"))
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | eval controller_iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_bus_iops, StorageBusIOPS, iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(outstanding_io, OutstandingIo, queue_depth, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(max_queue_depth, MaxQueueDepth, "64")), 10)
      | eval controller_latency_us=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(avg_latency_us, AvgLatencyUs, latency_us, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_throughput_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(adapter_throughput_mbps, AdapterThroughputMbps, smb_throughput_mbps, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(link_speed_mbps, LinkSpeedMbps, speed_mbps, "0")), 10)
      | fields cluster_id node_id vendor _time controller_iops controller_queue_depth controller_queue_max controller_latency_us vnic_throughput_mbps vnic_link_speed_mbps ]
| eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_depth, "0")), 10)
| eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_max, "128")), 10)
| eval controller_queue_util_pct=if((controller_queue_max>0), round((100.0*controller_queue_depth)/controller_queue_max, 2), null())
| eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_link_speed_mbps, "0")), 10)
| eval vnic_throughput_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_throughput_mbps, "0")), 10)
| eval vnic_link_util_pct=if((vnic_link_speed_mbps>0), round((100.0*vnic_throughput_mbps)/vnic_link_speed_mbps, 2), null())
| where (isnotnull(cluster_id)) AND (isnotnull(node_id)) AND (cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER") AND (len(toString(node_id))>0)
| bin span=5m _time
| stats avg(controller_queue_util_pct) AS qutil avg(vnic_link_util_pct) AS lutil avg(controller_latency_us) AS clat max(controller_iops) AS ciops BY cluster_id node_id vendor _time
| sort 0 cluster_id node_id vendor _time
| streamstats window=288 current=t global=f avg(qutil) AS queue_trend_24h avg(lutil) AS link_trend_24h BY cluster_id node_id vendor
| eval saturation_trend_score=round(((queue_trend_24h + link_trend_24h) / 2.0), 2)
| stats latest(saturation_trend_score) AS saturation_trend_score perc95(qutil) AS controller_queue_p95_pct perc99(qutil) AS controller_queue_p99_pct perc95(lutil) AS link_util_p95_pct perc99(lutil) AS link_util_p99_pct perc99(clat) AS controller_latency_p99_us max(ciops) AS controller_iops BY cluster_id node_id vendor
| eventstats avg(controller_queue_p99_pct) AS cluster_mean_queue_p99 avg(link_util_p99_pct) AS cluster_mean_link_p99 BY cluster_id vendor
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor [| inputlookup hci_controller_owner | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id) | eval vendor=toString(vendor) | eval target_max_controller_queue_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_controller_queue_pct, "85")), 10) | eval target_max_link_util_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_link_util_pct, "80")), 10) | eval target_max_p99_latency_us=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_p99_latency_us, "1500000")), 10) | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")) | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_max_controller_queue_pct target_max_link_util_pct target_max_p99_latency_us change_freeze_until ]
| eval tq=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_controller_queue_pct, "85")), 10)
| eval tl=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_link_util_pct, "80")), 10)
| eval tu=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_p99_latency_us, "1500000")), 10)
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND (isnotnull(ft)) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case(( (isnotnull(controller_queue_p99_pct)) AND (isnotnull(link_util_p99_pct)) AND (isnotnull(controller_latency_p99_us)) AND (controller_queue_p99_pct>=tq) AND (link_util_p99_pct>=tl) AND (controller_latency_p99_us>=tu) AND (saturation_trend_score>=65) ), "critical", ( (controller_queue_p99_pct>=(tq-8)) AND (link_util_p99_pct>=(tl-8)) AND (controller_latency_p99_us>=(0.85*tu)) ), "high", ( (controller_queue_p99_pct>=(0.82*tq)) OR (link_util_p99_pct>=(0.82*tl)) OR (controller_latency_p99_us>=(0.72*tu)) ), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0!="critical"), "info", sev0)
| table vendor cluster_id node_id controller_queue_p95_pct controller_queue_p99_pct link_util_p95_pct link_util_p99_pct controller_latency_p99_us saturation_trend_score cluster_mean_queue_p99 cluster_mean_link_p99 controller_iops severity owner_team change_freeze_until freeze_active
description: Normalizes vSAN, AOS, HyperFlex, and Azure Stack HCI feeds into one per-node schema for storage-controller queue utilization, controller-facing latency in microseconds, IOPS pressure, and storage-network throughput versus negotiated link speed, then applies percentile and twenty-four-hour rolling trend math with cluster-wide eventstats context so operators see controller-and-pipe saturation before cluster aggregate IOPS implies a problem, explicitly separate from UC-19.2.3 latency SLO tracking, UC-19.2.8 steady-state skew, UC-19.2.29 Nutanix CVM uplink specialization, and UC-19.2.30 vSAN resync backlog depth.
value: Hot controllers and full storage-network legs show up as queue depth hugging maximums and NIC utilization percentiles climbing long before a capacity dashboard declares the cluster busy. Publishing one multisearch alert gives platform, network, and application owners a shared language for when to rebalance guests, add cache, fix bonded uplinks, or widen fabric headroom, and it prevents wasted investigations that only watched cluster-level IOPS or average latency.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how crowded the storage controllers and data-network pipes are on each machine in your shared cluster, using percentile math so brief spikes do not wake people overnight. When one machine stays near its ceiling while neighbors stay calm, we give your team a clear row to fix that path before guests feel the slowdown.
implementation: Deploy the five ingestion paths or a subset, publish hci_controller_owner.csv with transforms, install the comment macro if missing, paste the multisearch SPL into a fifteen-minute saved search, route severity tiers to queues, add dashboards for heatmap and trend panels, and optionally join snmp:if:counters for discard corroboration.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

This use case is the controller-and-pipe saturation lane for hyperconverged estates. It measures per-node storage adapter and storage-network pressure with percentile math so a sixty-second spike does not page an on-call rotation. The customer outcome is early detection when a single node’s controller queue or uplink approaches its ceiling while cluster-wide IOPS still looks comfortable, which is a different question than guest-visible latency percentiles in UC-19.2.3, workload distribution skew in UC-19.2.8, Nutanix CVM uplink bandwidth specialization in UC-19.2.29, or vSAN component resync backlog depth in UC-19.2.30.

Multisearch rationale: each vendor exposes controller queue depth, adapter latency, IOPS, and NIC throughput on different JSON paths and cadences. One flat OR search across every sourcetype without field normalization produces silent nulls after a Technical Add-on upgrade because a key moved from snake_case to camelCase. Four explicit subsearches keep each arm reviewable, let you throttle a heavy Prism poller without touching a VMware stanza, and force the outer statistics to stay identical across stacks.

Primary sourcetypes and feeds, cited again in dataSources and Step 1: vSphere path uses Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder vmware:perf for host counters consistent with disk.queueLatency.average, disk.deviceLatency.average, disk.maxQueueDepth.latest, net.usage.average, net.bytesTx.average, and net.bytesRx.average naming patterns in your metric dimension field, plus PowerCLI scheduled export vmware:vsphere:host:hba for Get-VMHostStorage outstanding I/O fields and vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic for Get-VMHostNetworkAdapter link speed, MTU, and throughput when you prefer export granularity over perf aggregation. Nutanix path uses Splunk_TA_nutanix nutanix:host and nutanix:cluster_metrics, plus custom HEC sourcetypes nutanix:host:throughput and nutanix:host:nic aligned to Prism Element REST /PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/hosts/{uuid}/stats for controller_avg_io_latency_usecs, controller_num_iops, controller_io_bandwidth_kBps, and per-interface network counters. HyperFlex path prefers Splunk Add-on for Cisco HyperFlex, otherwise HX Connect REST /rest/clusters/{cid}/nodes/stats modeled as hyperflex:node:controller and hyperflex:node:vnic with fields such as controller_iops_max, controller_iops_used, controller_queue_depth, vnic_throughput_mbps, and vnic_link_speed_mbps. Azure Stack HCI path uses PowerShell scheduled export Get-StorageBusBindingClient, Get-StorageBusCache, Get-NetAdapterStatistics, and Get-SmbServerNetworkInterface to HEC sourcetypes azure_stack_hci:storagebus and azure_stack_hci:vnic with stable keys for outstanding I/O, average latency in microseconds where available, throughput, and negotiated speed.

Secondary optional corroboration: Splunk Connect for SNMP maps top-of-rack switch storage VLAN interfaces to sourcetype snmp:if:counters using ifHCInOctets, ifHCOutOctets, ifInDiscards, and ifOutDiscards so you can compare a rising node-side utilization story to switch-side slopes. SNMP never replaces the node-normalized multisearch for paging; it answers whether discards or an upstream choke are co-moving with the node trend.

Indexes: converge on index hci for multivendor merges if governance allows, otherwise OR hci with vmware, nutanix, cisco, and microsoft indexes inside each arm. Publish a single HTTP Event Collector token per collector team when blast-radius policy demands separation; rotate secrets on a fixed calendar; prefer acknowledgement on forwarders that honor indexed acknowledgement for metric streams.

Lookup file hci_controller_owner.csv lives in lookups with transforms.conf stanza hci_controller_owner, min_matches zero, case sensitivity aligned to cluster_id and vendor spelling in CMDB. Documented columns for hci_controller_owner.csv: cluster_id, vendor, owner_team, target_max_controller_queue_pct, target_max_link_util_pct, target_max_p99_latency_us, change_freeze_until. Optional site_id or business_service columns may be joined in customer extensions without renaming the core headers.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

Create or extend a wrapper application such as TA_hci_controller_saturation with local props, transforms, and HEC stanzas so vendor defaults stay untouched. props.conf should set SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false, raise TRUNCATE for large JSON lines, and apply INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json where events arrive as single-line JSON from HTTP Event Collector. Time fields should be emitted UTC; store poll_utc alongside vendor payloads to simplify forensics across regions.

VMware and vSAN: enable Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder host performance samples into vmware:perf with explicit metric naming documented beside your inventory host naming standard. Schedule PowerCLI on a jump host to post vmware:vsphere:host:hba and vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic exports at five-minute intervals so OutstandingIO and negotiated megabit speed exist even when a perf rollup omits an adapter-level nuance. Record the exact Connect-VIServer identity in the automation vault and keep cmdlet output keys stable across PowerCLI upgrades.

Nutanix: install Splunk_TA_nutanix on a collector with Prism reachability. Enable modular inputs for nutanix:host and nutanix:cluster_metrics per vendor guidance. Layer a read-only Prism Element poller that merges per-host stats and NIC inventory into nutanix:host:throughput or split throughput versus nutanix:host:nic if you want smaller events. Coalesce-friendly field names at ingest reduce night-time SPL edits when a minor release renames JSON keys.

HyperFlex: prefer the Splunk Add-on for Cisco HyperFlex when entitlement and architecture reviews complete. Otherwise implement HX Connect REST read-only polling with explicit API version pins in git. Map controller_iops_max as a ceiling when you compute utilization against used IOPS, and keep vnic_link_speed_mbps as the denominator for percent-of-link math on each node.

Azure Stack HCI: run scheduled PowerShell that wraps Storage Bus cache and binding clients, SMB interface statistics, and adapter counters in JSON with keys tested against a lab indexer for line breaking. Separate storage_bus and vnic sourcetype routing keeps parser risk isolated.

SC4SNMP optional: configure SNMPv3 credentials on the network operational vault, poll storage-facing ToR interfaces at sixty-second intervals, and keep sourcetype snmp:if:counters consistent with your NetOps app so dashboard joins only require ifDescr normalization.

Vendor-by-vendor mapping table for the unified schema used in Step 2: cluster_id is the customer canonical cluster key across CMDB and UC-19.2.16 failure-domain work; node_id is hypervisor host identity or vendor serial per your standard; controller_iops is read-plus-write style IOPS or vendor-used IOPS where that is the meaningful pressure signal; controller_queue_depth is outstanding I/O from HBA or vendor adapter queue; controller_queue_max is maximum queue depth or a governance max derived from controller_iops_max on HyperFlex when raw depth is unavailable; controller_latency_us is microseconds for controller-facing latency; vnic_throughput_mbps is the combined megabit-per-second story normalized to Mbps using byte counters times eight divided by one million when you start from bytes per second; vnic_link_speed_mbps is negotiated or catalog link speed on the storage-facing path; vnic_link_util_pct is one hundred times throughput divided by link speed on the same node, never an absolute Mbps compared across mixed 10G and 25G nodes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Paste the SPL from the spl field into Search, confirm non-zero rows on a known cluster, then Save As Alert hci_uc_19_2_11_controller_pipe_sat with cron */15 * * * * and dispatch earliest=-25h@h latest=@h so the twenty-four hour streamstats window is meaningful. Route critical to the platform bridge queue, high to infrastructure chat with owner_team mention, warning to email with ticket template, info to dashboard-only consumers unless you are trending saturation for quarterly capacity reviews. Throttle duplicates by cluster_id plus node_id plus vendor for sixty minutes unless severity escalates.

Severity logic intentionally demands concurrent percentile pressure on controller queue utilization, link utilization, and controller latency microseconds plus a non-trivial saturation_trend_score so a single missing NIC export cannot manufacture a false critical. Extend coalesce lists when VMware exposes a new perf counter synonym; prefer FIELDALIAS at parse time for stable models.

Fenced SPL for runbooks mirrors the spl JSON field:

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.11 HCI storage-controller queue AND storage-network pipe saturation: multisearch normalizes vSAN vmware:perf and PowerCLI vmware:vsphere:host:hba and vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic, Nutanix nutanix:host nutanix:cluster_metrics nutanix:host:throughput nutanix:host:nic, HyperFlex hyperflex:node:controller hyperflex:node:vnic, Azure Stack HCI azure_stack_hci:storagebus azure_stack_hci:vnic. Tunables: hci_controller_owner.csv columns target_max_controller_queue_pct target_max_link_util_pct target_max_p99_latency_us; owner_team; change_freeze_until freeze demotes non-critical to info. Window earliest=-25h@h latest=@h feeds perc99 AND streamstats window=288 on 5m bins (~24h rolling means). Alert cron */15 * * * *. Coalesce() covers camelCase drift. Relates to UC-19.2.3 guest-visible latency, UC-19.2.8 placement skew, UC-19.2.29 Nutanix CVM uplink slice, UC-19.2.30 vSAN component resync depth.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:perf" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:hba" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id, host_name, hostName, name, hostname, host, entity, HypervisorHostname))
      | eval vendor="vsan"
      | eval mkey=lower(toString(coalesce(metric, metric_name, MetricName, counter, "")))
      | eval vnum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, val, latest, "0")), 10)
      | eval disk_ql_ms=if(match(mkey, "disk\.queueLatency|queuelatency"), vnum, null())
      | eval disk_dl_ms=if(match(mkey, "disk\.deviceLatency|devicelatency"), vnum, null())
      | eval disk_mqd=if(match(mkey, "maxQueueDepth|maxqueuedepth"), vnum, null())
      | eval net_brx=if(match(mkey, "bytesrx|bytesRx"), vnum, null())
      | eval net_btx=if(match(mkey, "bytestx|bytesTx"), vnum, null())
      | eval disk_rd_iops=if(match(mkey, "numberreadaveraged|numberReadAveraged"), vnum, null())
      | eval disk_wr_iops=if(match(mkey, "numberwriteaveraged|numberWriteAveraged"), vnum, null())
      | eval hba_qd=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:hba", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(OutstandingIO, outstandingIo, outstanding_io, hba_outstanding_io, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval hba_qmax=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:hba", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(QueueDepth, queueDepth, max_queue_depth, hba_queue_max, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval ls_mbps=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(LinkSpeedMbps, linkSpeedMbps, link_speed_mbps, negotiated_speed_mbps, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vrx=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rx_mbps, rxMbps, net_rx_mbps, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vtx=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host:vmnic", tonumber(tostring(coalesce(tx_mbps, txMbps, net_tx_mbps, "0")), 10), null())
      | bin span=1m _time
      | stats max(disk_ql_ms) AS dql_ms max(disk_dl_ms) AS ddl_ms max(disk_mqd) AS dmqd max(net_brx) AS nbrx max(net_btx) AS nbtx max(disk_rd_iops) AS driops max(disk_wr_iops) AS dwiops max(hba_qd) AS hba_qd max(hba_qmax) AS hba_qmax max(ls_mbps) AS lsmb max(vrx) AS vrx max(vtx) AS vtx BY cluster_id node_id vendor _time
      | eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(hba_qd, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(hba_qmax, dmqd, controller_queue_max, "128")), 10)
      | eval controller_latency_us=coalesce(if((ddl_ms>0), ddl_ms*1000.0, null()), if((dql_ms>0), dql_ms*1000.0, null()))
      | eval kbps=coalesce(nbrx, 0)+coalesce(nbtx, 0)
      | eval vnic_throughput_mbps=round(coalesce(vrx, 0)+coalesce(vtx, 0)+if((kbps>0), (kbps*8.0)/1000.0, 0), 4)
      | eval controller_iops=coalesce(driops, 0)+coalesce(dwiops, 0)
      | eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=lsmb
      | fields cluster_id node_id vendor _time controller_iops controller_queue_depth controller_queue_max controller_latency_us vnic_throughput_mbps vnic_link_speed_mbps ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:host" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster_metrics" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host:throughput" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host:nic") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(host_uuid, uuid, hostUuid, name, host_name, node_id))
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | eval controller_iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_num_iops, controllerNumIops, controller_iops, iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_latency_us=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_avg_io_latency_usecs, controllerAvgIoLatencyUsecs, "0")), 10)
      | eval kbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_io_bandwidth_kBps, controllerIoBandwidthKBps, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_depth, controllerQueueDepth, io_queue_depth, ctrl_qd, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_max, controllerQueueMax, max_io_queue_depth, "128")), 10)
      | eval _tput=if((kbps>0), round((kbps/1024.0)*8.0, 4), null())
      | eval vnic_throughput_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_throughput_mbps, network_throughput_mbps, _tput, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(link_speed_mbps, linkSpeedMbps, negotiated_speed_mbps, nic_speed_mbps, "0")), 10)
      | fields cluster_id node_id vendor _time controller_iops controller_queue_depth controller_queue_max controller_latency_us vnic_throughput_mbps vnic_link_speed_mbps ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) (sourcetype="hyperflex:node:controller" OR sourcetype="hyperflex:node:vnic") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id, nodeId, node_name, host_name, serialNumber, serial))
      | eval vendor="hyperflex"
      | eval controller_iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_iops_used, controllerIopsUsed, controller_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_depth, controllerQueueDepth, ctrl_queue_depth, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_iops_max, controllerIopsMax, ctrl_queue_max, "128")), 10)
      | eval controller_latency_us=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_latency_us, controllerLatencyUs, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_throughput_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_throughput_mbps, vnicThroughputMbps, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_link_speed_mbps, vnicLinkSpeedMbps, "0")), 10)
      | fields cluster_id node_id vendor _time controller_iops controller_queue_depth controller_queue_max controller_latency_us vnic_throughput_mbps vnic_link_speed_mbps ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:storagebus" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:vnic") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_name, NodeName, NetAdapterName, ComputerName, name, "UNKNOWN_NODE"))
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | eval controller_iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_bus_iops, StorageBusIOPS, iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(outstanding_io, OutstandingIo, queue_depth, "0")), 10)
      | eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(max_queue_depth, MaxQueueDepth, "64")), 10)
      | eval controller_latency_us=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(avg_latency_us, AvgLatencyUs, latency_us, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_throughput_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(adapter_throughput_mbps, AdapterThroughputMbps, smb_throughput_mbps, "0")), 10)
      | eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(link_speed_mbps, LinkSpeedMbps, speed_mbps, "0")), 10)
      | fields cluster_id node_id vendor _time controller_iops controller_queue_depth controller_queue_max controller_latency_us vnic_throughput_mbps vnic_link_speed_mbps ]
| eval controller_queue_depth=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_depth, "0")), 10)
| eval controller_queue_max=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(controller_queue_max, "128")), 10)
| eval controller_queue_util_pct=if((controller_queue_max>0), round((100.0*controller_queue_depth)/controller_queue_max, 2), null())
| eval vnic_link_speed_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_link_speed_mbps, "0")), 10)
| eval vnic_throughput_mbps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vnic_throughput_mbps, "0")), 10)
| eval vnic_link_util_pct=if((vnic_link_speed_mbps>0), round((100.0*vnic_throughput_mbps)/vnic_link_speed_mbps, 2), null())
| where (isnotnull(cluster_id)) AND (isnotnull(node_id)) AND (cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER") AND (len(toString(node_id))>0)
| bin span=5m _time
| stats avg(controller_queue_util_pct) AS qutil avg(vnic_link_util_pct) AS lutil avg(controller_latency_us) AS clat max(controller_iops) AS ciops BY cluster_id node_id vendor _time
| sort 0 cluster_id node_id vendor _time
| streamstats window=288 current=t global=f avg(qutil) AS queue_trend_24h avg(lutil) AS link_trend_24h BY cluster_id node_id vendor
| eval saturation_trend_score=round(((queue_trend_24h + link_trend_24h) / 2.0), 2)
| stats latest(saturation_trend_score) AS saturation_trend_score perc95(qutil) AS controller_queue_p95_pct perc99(qutil) AS controller_queue_p99_pct perc95(lutil) AS link_util_p95_pct perc99(lutil) AS link_util_p99_pct perc99(clat) AS controller_latency_p99_us max(ciops) AS controller_iops BY cluster_id node_id vendor
| eventstats avg(controller_queue_p99_pct) AS cluster_mean_queue_p99 avg(link_util_p99_pct) AS cluster_mean_link_p99 BY cluster_id vendor
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor [| inputlookup hci_controller_owner | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id) | eval vendor=toString(vendor) | eval target_max_controller_queue_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_controller_queue_pct, "85")), 10) | eval target_max_link_util_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_link_util_pct, "80")), 10) | eval target_max_p99_latency_us=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_p99_latency_us, "1500000")), 10) | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")) | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_max_controller_queue_pct target_max_link_util_pct target_max_p99_latency_us change_freeze_until ]
| eval tq=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_controller_queue_pct, "85")), 10)
| eval tl=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_link_util_pct, "80")), 10)
| eval tu=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_p99_latency_us, "1500000")), 10)
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND (isnotnull(ft)) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case(( (isnotnull(controller_queue_p99_pct)) AND (isnotnull(link_util_p99_pct)) AND (isnotnull(controller_latency_p99_us)) AND (controller_queue_p99_pct>=tq) AND (link_util_p99_pct>=tl) AND (controller_latency_p99_us>=tu) AND (saturation_trend_score>=65) ), "critical", ( (controller_queue_p99_pct>=(tq-8)) AND (link_util_p99_pct>=(tl-8)) AND (controller_latency_p99_us>=(0.85*tu)) ), "high", ( (controller_queue_p99_pct>=(0.82*tq)) OR (link_util_p99_pct>=(0.82*tl)) OR (controller_latency_p99_us>=(0.72*tu)) ), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0!="critical"), "info", sev0)
| table vendor cluster_id node_id controller_queue_p95_pct controller_queue_p99_pct link_util_p95_pct link_util_p99_pct controller_latency_p99_us saturation_trend_score cluster_mean_queue_p99 cluster_mean_link_p99 controller_iops severity owner_team change_freeze_until freeze_active
```

Step 3 — Validate

vSphere HTML5 UI: open Datacenter, Cluster, Monitor, Performance, Advanced, pick the host, add chart metrics for Storage Adapter or advanced disk charts that include queue and device latency families, and compare slope direction to the Splunk five-minute bins for the same node_id during a quiet window. Hardware tab HBA inventory should reconcile OutstandingIO peaks with controller_queue_depth if PowerCLI export is enabled.

Prism Element: browse Hardware, Host, Performance, Controller, and read IOPS plus bandwidth lines next to Splunk nutanix arms for the same fifteen-minute horizon. NIC pages validate negotiated speed used as vnic_link_speed_mbps.

HX Connect: Cluster, Activity, Network and controller sections should show co-moving throughput and queue indicators with hyperflex sourcetypes.

Windows Admin Center on Azure Stack HCI: Cluster, Storage, Activity plus network adapters clarify whether Storage Spaces Direct paths align with azure_stack_hci exports.

Stress proof in a lab only: run vdbench or fio with duration under thirty minutes, queue depth tuned below hardware destructive levels, and throughput set to approach but not exceed negotiated link speed on a dedicated test VLAN. Expect controller_queue_p95_pct and link_util_p95_pct to rise together, then fall after job end without guest SLO breach. Pair the test window with optional snmp:if:counters on the storage ToR to confirm the slope direction matches egress.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboards: per-node controller queue heatmap using controller_queue_p99_pct as color; per-node link utilization timechart of link_util_p95_pct; saturated nodes table sorted by severity then saturation_trend_score; cluster roll-up panel charting cluster_mean_queue_p99 alongside per-node outliers to show single-node domination versus cluster-wide heat.

Runbook actions: if one node breaches while siblings stay cool, evacuate noisy guests, verify HBA firmware, inspect NVMe queue depth settings versus RAID HBA limits, validate NIC bonding and switch port channels, and consider cache tier expansion when controller pressure is sustained. If many nodes co-breach, escalate architecture review for fabric oversubscription rather than endless local tuning. Tie UC-19.2.3 for latency-first guest stories, UC-19.2.8 for placement skew, UC-19.2.29 when Nutanix CVM uplink specialization is the sharper lens, UC-19.2.30 when vSAN resync backlog explains correlated pressure.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

Case one: vendor field rename after firmware upgrade returns null controller_queue_max. Mitigation: capture fieldsummary into git, extend coalesce lists in the multisearch arm, add FIELDALIAS stanzas, and re-run lab validation before promoting.

Case two: legitimate burst loads such as backup windows or synthetic training batches inflate percent momentarily. Mitigation: trust perc99 over raw max for paging and require saturation_trend_score in severity to dampen bursts.

Case three: mixed link-speed clusters where some nodes negotiate ten gigabit and others twenty five. Mitigation: keep vnic_link_util_pct as percent-of-self negotiated speed, never compare raw Mbps across nodes in the alert text.

Case four: MTU mismatch across storage VLAN endpoints fragments packets and can resemble link saturation in throughput counters while effective goodput is poor. Mitigation: correlate jumbo frame policy on ESXi vmknics, HyperFlex vnics, Nutanix bridges, SMB adapters, and switch SVIs before blaming pure capacity.

Case five: VMware storage vMotion or Distributed Resource Scheduler concurrent migrations concentrate IO on corridor nodes. Mitigation: overlay vmware:vc:event migration counts and maintenance flags, and annotate change windows using change_freeze_until.

Case six: switch-side discards visible in snmp:if:counters without matching node-side link_util spikes often imply cable, optics, or buffer issues upstream rather than a single controller queue; open a network bridge ticket with interface graphs.

Supplement 01 — governance hygiene: keep hci_controller_owner.csv rows version-controlled with change tickets; retest Prism and vCenter field paths after quarterly upgrades; archive one screenshot pair (vendor console and Splunk row) for each critical incident review; document NVMe queue-depth planning assumptions separately from SCSI HBA defaults; ensure ROBO exclusions remain documented so operators do not expect sibling baselines on single-node sites.

Extended notes for platform engineering: when Splunk major versions upgrade, re-run saved-search acceleration reports for this alert and confirm multisearch dispatch skew stays under organizational SLOs. When Prism Element and Prism Central both exist, prefer Element time bases for nutanix arms to avoid double-counting delayed central roll-ups. When vCenter perf intervals change from five minutes to two minutes, adjust bin span in optional forks only if license teams approve higher event volume. When storage QoS policies are introduced broadly, correlate guests under QoS caps with unexpectedly low link_util while controller_queue remains high. When Intersight orchestrates HyperFlex updates, freeze fields in hci_controller_owner should track the published change window so expected saturation during transitions does not flood pages. When Azure Stack HCI clusters stretch across sites, cluster_id must encode site disambiguation or duplicate natural keys will merge unrelated nodes in stats. When SNMP community or SNMPv3 auth rotates, update SC4SNMP secrets before dashboards show false flat lines that look like clean pipes. When forwarders use indexed extractions on wide JSON, monitor field quota warnings and split sourcetypes if KV growth threatens search performance. When a lab cluster shares names with production, enforce index-level routing tags so synthetic benchmarks never blend into production governance lookups. When ITSI imports KPIs from this search, map entity titles to node_id and cluster_id exactly as CMDB spells them to avoid pseudo-duplicate services. When SOAR playbooks open tickets, include vendor, severity, saturation_trend_score, and deep links to vendor consoles that operators can reach without extra VPN hops. When auditing evidence packs, attach the hci_controller_owner row revision hash next to the Splunk export for traceability. When network teams replace optics on storage ToR, schedule a short verification window where snmp:if:counters slopes are watched beside link_util. When storage teams add NVMe replaceable units, update controller_queue_max sourcing because firmware may expose deeper default queues than the prior generation. When VMware introduces new counter families for ESA architectures, extend the coalesce list after reading release notes rather than disabling the UC. When Nutanix enables data-at-rest encryption broadly, expect modest controller latency shifts; retarget target_max_p99_latency_us with workload SMEs. When Cisco HyperFlex upgrades change REST field paths, pin HX Connect versions in the automation repo alongside Splunk app versions. When Windows patches alter Storage Bus signatures, re-validate PowerShell exporter keys against a canary node before fleet rollout. When multilingual operations centers share dashboards, translate only panel titles while keeping field keys English for SPL portability. When zero-trust proxies sit between collectors and vendor APIs, document latency budgets so polls still complete within their intervals. When index retention shrinks below ten days, reduce reliance on long streamstats windows or accept approximate scoring with explicit caveats in the runbook.
visualization: Heatmap of controller_queue_p99_pct by node, timechart of link_util_p95_pct and saturation_trend_score, sortable alert table projecting vendor cluster_id node_id percentiles severity owner_team, optional side panel joining snmp:if:counters discard deltas on storage ToR ports.
evidence: Saved search hci_uc_19_2_11_controller_pipe_sat on fifteen-minute cadence, version-controlled lookups/hci_controller_owner.csv, quarterly CSV or PDF exports of the final table to a restricted evidence index with Splunk job links, and paired vendor console screenshots for sampled critical rows.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380)
Microsoft Learn — Azure Stack HCI overview

knownFalsePositives: Coordinated rolling hypervisor firmware waves can rotate elevated controller percentiles host by host without a defect. Storage maintenance that pauses one path can briefly stack outstanding I/O on the remaining path even when capacity remains healthy. Third-party replication appliances pinned beside HCI hosts may load vnics that appear saturated while guest latency stays acceptable until policy changes. Acceptance clusters running IO micro-benchmark suites on a schedule will sit intentionally near ceilings and should point at alternate governance thresholds.
exclusions: Remote-office single-node stacks with no sibling baseline for percentile context. Nested hypervisor labs where counters are not representative. Clusters that multiplex management and storage on one physical NIC without a documented separation story. Hosts whose primary NIC carries mostly user-facing VLAN traffic instead of storage replication. Benchmark environments that intentionally pin storage controllers for acceptance testing.
hardware: Targets VMware vSAN ReadyNode class platforms including Dell PowerEdge, HPE ProLiant, and Lenovo ThinkSystem generations; Nutanix NX, XC, HX, DX, and UCS-C qualified nodes; Cisco HyperFlex HX Series; Azure Stack HCI validated systems spanning Dell, HPE, Lenovo, Fujitsu, and Cisco rack footprints. Designs commonly isolate storage traffic on ten, twenty five, or one hundred gigabit Ethernet. NVMe back-end devices expose deeper queue depths than legacy SCSI HBAs, and RAID HBA versus passthrough HBA-330 class paths change how guest IOPS translate into adapter outstanding I/O, so align controller_queue_max sourcing with what the hardware actually exports.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — trellis KPIs on saturation_trend_score and controller percentiles

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab index, synthesize five-minute bins where qutil and lutil percentiles exceed hci_controller_owner thresholds for one node_id while peers stay low; verify severity reaches high or critical with freeze_active zero and saturation_trend_score non-trivial.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Populate change_freeze_until with a future ISO timestamp for the matching cluster_id and vendor, reload the lookup, and confirm severity demotes to info for rows that would otherwise be warning while percentile columns remain visible for audit.
